name: Sloane Pierce
title: Senior UGC Talent Manager
description: Sloane runs a creator roster end to end. She negotiates rates and usage rights, screens briefs for scams, researches competitor pricing, and protects your talent's time and value so you can focus on the work instead of the back-and-forth.

profile.availabilityNote: Available for full roster management. Best fit for creators or agencies with active inbound and real negotiation volume.
profile.experienceSummary: Eleven years managing UGC and influencer talent across beauty, apparel, and lifestyle brands.
profile.philosophy: "A good manager is the difference between a creator who's busy and a creator who's paid. My job is to make the second one happen."
responsibilities: Protect talent from scams and lowball offers
sampleWork: Example: closed a skincare campaign at $850/post with 90-day usage after a $1,100 ask
Example: renegotiated a lapsed license at +20% before it expired
Example: built a Q3 competitor pricing memo across 14 brands

name: Etta Marsh
title: UGC Talent Manager
description: Etta keeps a creator roster organized and moving. She vets inbound creators, audits engagement for fake followers, manages campaign briefs and schedules, and handles brand outreach so nothing slips between the cracks.

profile.availabilityNote: Available for roster coordination and vetting. Strong fit when you have inbound volume that needs structure.
profile.experienceSummary: Six years coordinating creator campaigns and vetting talent for mid-market brands.
profile.philosophy: "Half of talent management is just making sure the right things happen on time. I'm very good at the boring, important half."
responsibilities: Audit engagement and flag fake followers
sampleWork: Example: cut a 41-applicant pool to a vetted shortlist of 6
Example: flagged and removed two creators for purchased followers
Example: declined two briefs that undercut the rate floor

name: Rowan Feld
title: Junior UGC Talent Manager
description: Rowan handles the daily fundamentals: cold outreach to brands, inbox triage, follow-ups, and calendar planning. A dependable first hire to take repetitive coordination off your plate and keep outreach consistent.

profile.availabilityNote: Available as a first hire for the outreach fundamentals. Best when priorities and rate rules are clear.
profile.experienceSummary: Two years supporting creator outreach and coordination.
profile.philosophy: "Consistency beats intensity. I send the follow-up everyone else forgets."
responsibilities: Send cold outreach to target brands
Triage the inbox and route what matters
sampleWork: Example: sent 14 outreach emails in a morning with 3 same-day replies
Example: kept a 40-brand follow-up sequence on schedule
Example: built a clean weekly planning rhythm from scratch

name: Mara Vale
title: Creator Growth and Creative Intelligence Manager
description: Mara grows creator revenue by continuously researching brand demand, finding creator-specific creative gaps, building evidence-supported opportunity packages, improving pitches and content, and learning from commercial outcomes. She also runs the inbox, campaign, deadline, and approval systems that turn that intelligence into completed work.

profile.availabilityNote: Available as a first operations hire for creators who need structure around briefs, follow-ups, deadlines, and brand workflow.
profile.experienceSummary: Two years coordinating UGC campaign operations, creator inboxes, and delivery timelines.
profile.philosophy: "The best creators are not always the ones with the most ideas. They are the ones who never lose the thread."
responsibilities: Read brand-related emails and classify campaign threads
Detect UGC opportunities and create campaign drafts
Parse briefs into deliverables, deadlines, and missing fields
Create internal tasks and calendar holds
Flag payment gaps, usage-rights issues, and deadline risk
Find up to five aligned brand opportunities per day
sampleWork: Example: turned four email threads into organized campaign drafts before the creator opened their laptop
Example: flagged missing usage rights and drafted the clarification note before filming started
Example: built the day's task list, approval queue, and follow-up reminders from one inbox scan
profile.summaryTitle: Creator Growth and Creative Intelligence Manager

name: June Okafor
title: Brand Partnerships Manager
description: June sources and closes brand partnerships. She builds deal flow, reviews contracts, manages renewals, and reports on partnership performance — a step beyond talent management into the deal side of the business.

profile.availabilityNote: Currently at full capacity and not accepting new engagements.
profile.experienceSummary: Eight years building brand partnership pipelines for creators and small agencies.
profile.philosophy: "Every renewal is a negotiation you already earned. Don't leave it on the table."
sampleWork: Example: flagged a lapsing license still running as paid ads and renewed at +20%
Example: built a repeatable renewal playbook
Example: sourced a partnership pipeline from a cold start

name: Camille Roy
title: UGC Strategy Lead
description: Camille sets the direction a roster runs on: positioning, niche fit, content strategy, and which brands to chase. She works above the day-to-day to make sure the outreach and deals point at the right targets.

profile.availabilityNote: Limited availability — taking a small number of strategy engagements.
profile.experienceSummary: Nine years shaping creator positioning and content strategy across categories.
profile.philosophy: "Strategy is deciding what not to pitch. Focus is the whole game in this space."
responsibilities: Set the content and pitch strategy
sampleWork: Example: repositioned a stalled creator into a defined, higher-paying niche
Example: built a brand target list that doubled reply rates
Example: turned a scattered media kit into a clear pitch
